source_name: Code4rena Reserve Protocol Invitational Findings and Analysis Report (2023-07)
finding_title: ConvexStakingWrapper.sol after shutdown，rewards can be stolen
summary: After shutdown, checkpoints are stopped, leading to possible theft of rewards.
root_cause: reward shutdown accounting bug: after Convex shutdown, wrapper reward handling allows accumulated rewards to be claimed or redirected incorrectly
broken_invariants: rewards accrued before and after staking shutdown must remain attributable to the correct wrapper/share holders
shutdown paths must not leave reward tokens claimable by the wrong party
staking wrapper reward state must be synchronized before disabling or changing reward flows
attack_preconditions: the affected Reserve collateral/plugin/wrapper is deployed or configured in an RToken basket
the report-described oracle, reward, wrapper, or integration edge case is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, trader, or protocol participant able to trigger the affected pricing/trading/reward/withdrawal flow
trigger_actions: execute or wait for the report-described state transition
observe mispricing, reward misallocation, fund lock, or availability failure

impact.description: After shutdown, checkpoints are stopped, leading to possible theft of rewards.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with concrete affected code paths and impact narrative
common_false_positive_angles: verify the exact collateral plugin/wrapper is in scope and configured
check whether oracle freshness, decimals, or external integration assumptions differ in the current target
confirm the issue affects value movement, redemption availability, or reward ownership rather than a harmless view mismatch
mitigations: Still execute \_checkpoint function _checkpoint(address[2] memory _accounts) internal nonReentrant { //if shutdown, no longer checkpoint in case there are problems - if (isShutdown()) return; uint256 supply = _getTotalSupply(); uint256[2] memory depositedBalance; depositedBalance[0] = _getDepositedBalance(_accounts[0]); depositedBalance[1] = _getDepositedBalance(_accounts[1]); IRewardStaking(convexPool).getReward(address(this), true); _claimExtras(); uint256 rewardCount = rewards.length; for (uint256 i = 0; i < rewardCount; i++) { _calcRewardIntegral(i, _accounts, depositedBalance, supply, false); } } ### Assessed type Context

snippets.note: link preserved from Code4rena report or mitigation review
